Topic: Adding a Stern account onto Domain PC
Main Issues: Desktops are imaged specific for one users. Therefore only their Stern credentials would work. However, there are desktops that people shared ie. Front desk or student workers.
Resolution:
Figure out the location:
Go to Control Panel > Administrative Tools > Computer Management
Go to Local Users and Groups
Go to Groups
Go to the Administrative Group 
Select the option to Add a User
Enter the User's NetID
(To add anyone with a Stern ID add "Domain Users")
More Information:
You will need to log in as Administrator unless your account has Admin roles. IT-CSG members should have Admin roles so you can use your own credentials.
Right click 'This PC' -> Manage -> Groups -> Admins -> add new user
Related KBs: Desktop Support (Stern): Create a Local User Account in Windows

Main Issues: Either it is for first time users or returning users, there could be several reasons as to why they can't access their Stern account.
Resolution:
Check to make sure their Stern password is not expired on HATS.
If it is not expired, please make sure they are using their NetID as their username and Stern password.

Additionally, there may be times where the client has 'reset' their password but it didn't sync in our systems.
Please see when the last them they recall when they reset their password. 
If this does not match the expiration date on HATS, please have then reset their password once again.

Main Issues: When the client heads over to 'start.stern.nyu.edu' and they come across an error of information upon activating their Stern account. Depending on their role this may change.
Resolution:
Look up their NetID on HATS.
If they are a newly accepted student, their Stern account may not have been created. Please inform the client to wait 1-3 days for creation.

If they are students trying to register for a Stern course, they do not get one until they successfully enroll. 

If they are an alumni, please confirm their DOB on their HATS. Most commonly, Alumni may have their DOB to be 0000-00-00. Please verify the identity with the Alumni so you can get their DOB to be updated. Alternatively, the Alumni Office can confirm this.
Related KBs: Stern ID: Activating a Stern account,Stern ID: SIMON

Main Issues: This happens when the printer drivers of the smaller department printers are the specific ones. You should be able to search up the model of the printer to get the specific drivers or universal drivers.
Resolution: Most likely the driver that was installed is the wrong one which is why double sided/duplex is not working.
More Information: Search up the correct driver model to install the correct one.

2) Wipe the entire Chromebook back to factory and log in with their Stern Gmail first.
3) Disable MFA on HATS and then enable after they are able to log in.

Need the following information:
*Group Name:*
*Group email address:* __________________@stern.nyu.edu
*A brief description of the Group (for our records, e.g. A Group to facilitate communication between members of the eBoard):*
*A description of how you intend to use the Group (so we can configure it):*
*The Stern email addresses of anyone who should be made Group owners (there must be at least one):*

Resolution:
Look up the account on HATS first and Google Admin.
See whether Google Admin has indeed disabled it.
Then route the ticket over to eServices as they can confirm why the account has been locked.

eServices would send the ticket back with more information which you would then reply to the client to confirm the information.

Need to make sure they are the owner of the group

2) Send Mail As -> Stern Google Group
3) SMTP server: smtp.gmail.com
Port: 587
Username: ____@nyu.edu
Password: (Their NYU password)
Select TLS

Main Issues: Please double-check that the email did go to the client's Stern Gmail.
Resolution:
1) Collect a full trace of the suspicious email for eServices: https://support.google.com/mail/answer/29436?hl=en&visit_id=1-636462803869342866-2611480541&rd=1 
2) Once this is collected, route this over to eServices. Ask the client to refrain from interacting with the email.
3) Once eServices gets back with the suspicious email blocked, ask the client to mark the email as spam/phishing with Gmail's interface.

Please double-check that the email did go to the client's Stern Gmail.
If it went to their NYU Gmail, please have them forward the email over to 'phishing@nyu.edu'.
More Information: If it went to their NYU Gmail, please have them forward the email over to 'phishing@nyu.edu'.
Related KBs: Gmail@Stern: Stern Gmail compromised/hijacked receiving strange emails
